content: Na$$_2$$CO$$_3$$ is prepared by Solvay process but K$$_2$$CO$$_3$$ cannot be prepared by the same because

options.content: K$$_2$$CO$$_3$$ is highly soluble in H$$_2$$O

explanation:
$$\mathrm{KHCO}_3$$ is appreciably soluble in water, thus cannot be separated from reaction medium easily. So, it does not precipitates out like sodium bicarbonate. Hence, it is not prepared by solvay process.

$$\left(\mathrm{NH}_4\right) \mathrm{HCO}_3+\mathrm{KCl} \longrightarrow \underset{\substack{\text { Appreciably } \\ \text { soluble }}}{\mathrm{KHCO}_3(a q)}+\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{Cl}(a q)$$

content: A sample of MgCO3 is dissolved in dil. HCl and the solution is neutralized with ammonia and buffered with NH4Cl / NH4OH. Disodium hydrogen phosphate reagent is added to the resulting solution. A white precipitate is formed. What is the formula of the precipitate?
explanation:
The given reaction take place as follows:

$$\underset{(\mathrm{Magnesium\,carbonate})}{\mathrm{MgCO}_{3}}$$ $$\buildrel {\mathrm{dil\,2\,HCl}} \over \longrightarrow $$ $$\underset{(\mathrm{Magnesium\,chloride})}{\mathrm{MgCO}_{2}}\,+\,\mathrm{H_2O}\,+\,\mathrm{CO_2}$$

$$\underset{(\mathrm{Magnesium\,chloride})}{\mathrm{MgCl}_{2}}$$ + $$\mathrm{2NH_3}\,+\,\mathrm{2H_2O}\,\longrightarrow\,\mathrm{Mg(OH)_2}\,+\,\mathrm{2NH_4Cl}$$

$$\underset{(\mathrm{Magnesium\,hydroxide})}{\mathrm{Mg(OH)}_{2}}\,+\,\underset{(\mathrm{Disodium\,hydrogen\,phosphate})}{\mathrm{Na_2HPO_4}}\,+\,\mathrm{NH_4Cl}\,\longrightarrow\,\underset{(\mathrm{White\,ppt.})}{\mathrm{Mg(NH_4)PO_4}}\,+\,\mathrm{NH_3}\,+\,\mathrm{H_2O}\,+\,\mathrm{NaCl}$$

$$\therefore$$ The formula of white ppt. is $$\mathrm{Mg(NH_4)PO_4}$$.

content: To a solution of colourless sodium salt, a solution of lead nitrate was added to have a white precipitate which dissolves in warm water and reprecipitates on cooling. Which of the following acid radical is present in the salt?
explanation:
The given reaction take place as follows

$$\underset{(\mathrm{Sodium\,salt})}{\mathrm{2NaCl}}\,+\,\underset{(\mathrm{Lead\,nitrate})}{\mathrm{Pb(NO_3)}_{2}}\,\longrightarrow\,\underset{\mathrm{(White\,ppt.)\,lead\,chloride}}{\mathrm{PbCl}_{2}\downarrow}\,+\,\underset{(\mathrm{Sodium\,nitrate})}{\mathrm{2NaNO}_{3}}$$

$$\mathrm{PbCl_2}$$ is soluble in hot water and forms white ppt. in cold condition.

This show that $$\mathrm{Cl^-}$$ acid radical is present in the salt.

content:
Indicate the products (X) and (Y) in the following reactions

Na2S + nS(n = 1 $$-$$ 8) $$\to$$ (X)

Na2SO3 + S $$\to$$ (Y)
explanation:
Na2S + nS $$\to$$ Na2S(n + 1)

Na2SO3 + S $$\to$$ Na2S2O3

X = Na2S(n + 1)

Y = Na2S2O3

content: The white precipitate (Y), obtained on passing colorless and odourless gas (X) through an ammoniacal solution of NaCl, losses about 37% of its weight on heating and a white residue (Z) of basic nature is left. Identify (X), (Y) and (Z) from following sets.
explanation:
When CO2 is passed through ammoniacal solution of NaCl, sodium bicarbonate (NaHCO3) and (NH4Cl) are formed.

$$N{H_4}OH + NaCl + \mathop {C{O_2}}\limits_{(X)} \to \mathop {NaHC{O_3}}\limits_{Sodium\,bicarbonate\,(Y)} + \mathop {N{H_4}Cl}\limits_{Ammonium\,chloride} $$

On further heating NaHCO3, we get Na2CO3.

$$2NaHC{O_3} \to \mathop {N{a_2}C{O_3}}\limits_{Sodium\,\,carbonate\,(white\,ppt.)\,(Z)} + {H_2}O + C{O_2}$$

content: In the extraction of Ca by electro reduction of molten CaCl<sub>2</sub> some CaF<sub>2</sub> is added to the electrolyte for the following reason :

options.content: To keep the electrolyte in liquid state at temperature lower than the m.p. of CaCl<sub>2</sub>
To effect the electrolysis at lower voltage

explanation:
For the extraction of Ca by electro-reduction of molten CaCl2 some CaF2 is added to the electrolyte because CaF2 decreases the melting point of CaCl2. It keep the electrolyte in liquid state of temperature than the m.p. of CaCl2

So, option (a) is correct.

content: The compound, which evolves carbon dioxide on treatment with aqueous solution of sodium bicarbonate at 25$$^\circ$$C, is
explanation:
hydrolyses to form CH3COOH even at 25$$^\circ$$C, which subsequently reacts with NaHCO3 (sodium bicarbonate) present in the same medium to form carbon dioxide (CO2). The reaction involved is given below.

CH3COCl + NaHCO3 $$\to$$ CH3COONa + HCl + CO2

Thus, the option (b) is correct answer.

content: The reactive species in chlorine bleach is
explanation:
Chlorine bleach is NaOCl. The hypochlorite ion in chlorine bleach dissociates to give nascent oxygen as shown below.

OCl$$-$$ $$\to$$ [O] + Cl$$-$$

Therefore, the reactive species in chlorine bleach is OCl$$-$$.

So, the option (b) is correct.

content: Which of the following is least thermally stable?
explanation:
BeCO3 is least thermally stable. The thermal stability of carbonates increases down the group i.e. from Be to Ba.

$$\mathop {BeC{O_3}}\limits_{(523\,K)} < \mathop {MgC{O_3}}\limits_{(813\,K)} < \mathop {CaC{O_3}}\limits_{(1173\,K)} < \mathop {SrC{O_3}}\limits_{(1562\,K)} < \mathop {BaC{O_3}}\limits_{(1633\,K)} $$

BeCO3 is unstable to the extent that it is stable only in atmosphere of CO2. These carbonates show reversible decomposition in closed container.

MCO3 $$\rightleftharpoons$$ MO + CO2

More stable the oxide is formed, lesser will be stability of carbonates.

content: Which of the following solutions will turn violet when a drop of lime juice is added to it?

options.content: A solution mixture of KI and NaIO<sub>3</sub>
A solution mixture of NaI and KI
A solution mixture of KIO<sub>3</sub> and NaIO<sub>3</sub>

explanation:
Option B, a solution mixture of KI and NaIO$_3$, will turn violet when a drop of lime juice is added to it. This occurs due to the acid-catalyzed reaction between iodide ions (I$^-$) and iodate ions (IO$_3^-$) in the presence of an acidic environment like lime juice, producing elemental iodine ($I_2$), which imparts a violet color.

The underlying chemical reaction can be represented as:

$ 5 \text{I}^- + \text{IO}_3^- + 6 \text{H}^+ \rightarrow 3 \text{I}_2 + 3 \text{H}_2\text{O} $

In this reaction:

$ \text{I}^- $ is derived from KI.

$ \text{IO}_3^- $ is provided by NaIO$_3$.

$ \text{H}^+ $ ions are supplied by the acidic lime juice (which contains citric acid).

Therefore, the violet color results from the formation of iodine ($ \text{I}_2 $), indicating the occurrence of the reaction.

options.content: All B atoms are sp<sup>3</sup> hybridised.
There are two types of H present.

explanation: It has two 3-centre-2-electron bonds and four 2-centre-2-electron bonds.

content: During electrolysis of molten NaCl, some water was added. What will happen?

options.content: Some amount of caustic soda will be formed

explanation:
During electrolysis of molten NaCl, when water is added, the following statements are true

(a) false

(b) $$\because$$ Na + H2O $$\to$$ NaOH + $${1 \over 2}$$H2

and discharge potential of hydrogen is less than of sodium. So, H2 will evolve.

(c) Some amount of caustic soda (NaOH) will be formed. [As shown in (b)]

(d) Fire can likely take place because relations given in (b) is highly exothermic.

$$\therefore$$ (b), (c) and (d) are correct.
